content:
# Recommendations for iter-088

## Priority 1 — Confirm build gate

The 5-file CSILeg chain (Leg/Mid1/Mid2/Top/Aux) has no oleans yet. The next iter's primary task is observing whether the review-phase lake build produces oleans for all 5 files and CechToHigherDirectImage.

**Expected outcome (all at 46K HB/s pessimistic):**
- CSILeg: ~104s ✓
- CSILegMid1: ~122s ✓
- CSILegMid2: ~122s ✓
- CSILegTop: ~174s ✓
- CSILegAux: ~313s ✓ (highest risk — watch this one)
- CechToHigherDirectImage: depends on chain completing

If any of the above SIGTERMs, note which file and plan a further split targeting < 200s per module.

## Priority 2 — On green build: advance to polish

If lake build succeeds for the full chain:
- Stage → polish
- Run axiom-clean check (`#print axioms cech_computes_higherDirectImage`)
- Clean stale module docstrings, dead scaffolding lemmas (`pushPullLegIso`, `pushPull_leg_coherence`)
- Handle 4 orphan frontier nodes (tile_section_comparison, pushforward_commutes_restriction): these have `used-by 0`; either blueprint them or mark as dormant

## Priority 3 — Frontier nodes (Route B residuals)

Two frontier nodes have `used-by 0` (nothing depends on them):
- `lem:tile_section_comparison` (effort 5106)
- `lem:pushforward_commutes_restriction` (effort 1370)

These are NOT on the critical path to `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. Do NOT assign provers until the build gate clears and stage advances to polish. At polish stage, decide: prove them (Route B completion) or document as out-of-scope.

## Do NOT retry

- Building CSILeg as a single ~28M HB file (build wall confirmed at ~46K HB/s)
- Any approach that reintroduces sorries into the chain
- Dispatching provers for 0-sorry files (plan-validate will drop them → plan_validate_failed)

## Build timing reminder

At 46K HB/s (pessimistic confirmed iter-086): 10-min budget = ~600s per module.
LegAux at ~313s has ~2× safety margin but no guarantee of exact HB/s; if a future iter shows LegAux SIGTERM, split it (currently 234 lines → two ~117-line halves).
